mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding economic output and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors price in higher capacity utilization.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change versus SPX 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% indicates labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than consumer price inflation, real interest rates increase, tightening financial conditions and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between the change in real federal funds rate and SPX 60-day forward returns is not negative (i.e., >= 0) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year above 5%), stronger retail and food services sales indicate demand-pull inflation that forces the Fed to tighten policy, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During episodes when CPIAUCSL YoY inflation exceeds 5%, the correlation between RSAFS month-over-month change and SPX 20-day forward returns is not negative (i.e., >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong demand in the construction sector, consumer confidence, and future economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the very short term as markets react to the leading indicator.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of HOUST month-over-month change versus SPX 5-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, markets anticipate aggressive Fed tightening that raises the discount rate on equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of cases where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, the 20-day SPX return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production over three months while nonfarm payrolls continue to rise signals a lagging labor market that will soon correct, causing negative S&P 500 returns as the divergence resolves through slower employment growth.
failure_condition: In events where INDPRO declines over 3 months and PAYEMS rises in the same period, the majority of subsequent 20-day SPX returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in real retail sales (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) signals a collapse in consumer spending that erodes corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the economy enters a recession.
failure_condition: After real retail sales decline >2% MoM, the median 60-day SPX return is not negative, or the hit rate is below 55%.
